segments.s550-g25: The clause places the predicate before the subject, a common poetic/classical order: نشان بس بود = 'was a sufficient sign'. نشان (noun) is modified by بس 'enough', بود is the past copula 'was', and شهریار اردشیر names the subject (and can also be read as a vocative address 'O King Ardashir').
segments.s550-g26: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when'; از من سخن literally 'words from me' means 'what I say' or 'about me'; بشنوی is 2nd-person present-subjunctive/optative ('you hear') used in the temporal clause; یادگیر is an imperative 'learn'/'remember' directed at the addressee. Together: 'When you hear my words, learn.'
segments.s550-g9: اگر introduces a conditional 'if'. ز is the poetic preposition 'from/ because of' and combines with آهنی چرخ ('iron wheel') — چرخ here is the cosmic/wheel-of-fate sense. بگدازدت is an archaic verbal form with the 2nd-person enclitic -ت ('you') and expresses an irrealis/optative idea ('would melt/consume you'); the prefix ب- and the form place it in Ferdowsi's older style rather than modern tense-aspect marking.
segments.s550-g10: چو here is temporal 'when'. گشتی is an older second-person form meaning 'you become / you are made (old)' (Ferdowsi uses such short past/ perfective forms). کهن is the adjective 'old/ancient'. نیز 'also/too' links this clause to the previous conditional, and ننوازدت is the negative of نوازَدن with the 2nd-person enclitic -ت: 'it would not caress/spare you' (negative + archaic verbal ending, continuing the same impersonal/fateful subject as the previous clause).
segments.s550-g11: چو = 'when'. سرو دلارای is a noun + epithet ('cypress, the beloved'), with the epithet following the noun as is common in Persian poetry. گردد is the present/subjunctive/impersonal 'becomes' (گردد used for 'turns into / is made'), and به خم is a prepositional phrase 'into a curve/bend' describing how the cypress becomes.
segments.s550-g12: خروشان is an adjectival/adverbial participial form 'roaring/tumultuous' placed before the verb for emphasis. شود (another form of 'become') functions as the main verb applying to the following nominal phrase. نرگسان is a poetic plural/collective (literally 'narcissi' but here a conventional image for beautiful eyes), and دژم is a predicative adjective 'sullen/grim' describing their new state — word order is adjective(s) + verb + predicate noun/adjective.
segments.s550-g13: همان is the demonstrative 'that/the same' placed before چهره ('face'). The two following words ارغوان زعفران are color epithets in apposition (crimson/purple and saffron-colored) — poetic stacked adjectives modifying 'face'.
segments.s550-g14: سبک مردم functions as a genitival phrase 'the lightness / manner of the people' (literally 'people's lightness'). شاد is the predicate adjective 'happy/joyous' and گردَد is the impersonal/optative 'becomes'. گران is a predicate complement meaning 'heavy/precious/esteemed' placed after the verb; the line thus says that when the people's mood turns light and joyous, that face is considered precious — word order reflects poetic inversion.
segments.s550-g15: اگر ... و گر is a paired conditional/contrastive construction 'if ... or if / whether ... or'. شهریاری here names 'sovereignty / kingship' (i.e., being a ruler) and زیردست means 'one under (someone) / a subordinate'; the two nouns present the contrasting cases (ruler vs. subject).
segments.s550-g16: بجز 'except' introduces the exception phrase. خاک تیره 'dark earth/dust' is the exception (i.e., the only resting-place). نیابی is the 2nd-person form 'you will (not) find/attain' used here in the sense 'you will find (no) ...' governed by the بجز construction, and نشست is the noun 'seat/settling/rest' (the object — 'a place to sit/settle'). Together the clause means you will find no resting-place except dark earth.
segments.s550-g17: کجا is an interrogative/exclamatory particle ('where') used rhetorically; آن بزرگان is the nominal phrase 'those great ones' (آن as demonstrative + بزرگان plural). با تاج و تخت is a prepositional phrase 'with crown and throne' describing those great ones. The clause is elliptical (no copula like هستند) as is common in classical Persian poetry.
segments.s550-g18: Again کجا opens a rhetorical question; آن سواران is 'those horsemen/riders' and پیروزبخت is a compound adjective 'fortunate/prosperous (in victory)' modifying them. Poetic word order permits the adjective to follow the noun.
segments.s550-g19: کجا آن introduces another rhetorical question. خردمند is an adjective 'wise' modifying the following plural agent noun کندآوران (formed with the suffix -آوران, 'those who bring/make'); the compound functions as a plural substantive 'those wise bringers/makers'. This is an archaic/poetic agentive formation.
segments.s550-g20: کجا آن again opens the question; سرافراز is 'lofty/ triumphant' and جنگی is an adjective 'warlike' (here describing the type of leaders), joined by و to سران 'leaders/chiefs' (سران as the head noun). The adjectives precede or follow the noun flexibly in poetry.
segments.s550-g21: کجا آن again introduces the lament. گزیده is an adjective meaning 'chosen/elite' modifying نیاکان 'ancestors'. ما is a postposed possessive pronoun 'our' attached after the noun phrase (a normal Persian word order for possessor).
segments.s550-g22: کجا آن opens the parallel question; دلیران و پاکان is a coordinated noun phrase 'the brave and the pure' (plural nouns), and ما again follows as the possessive 'our', giving 'our brave and our pure ones.' The coordination is simple and poetic.
segments.s550-g23: همه is the indefinite subject 'all/everyone'. خاک دارند is literally 'have earth' with دارند present-tense 'have/possess'; بالین و خشت ('pillow and brick') are what they 'have' or what serves as their pillow/bed — the phrase expresses that everyone rests on earth and a simple brick. Word order is subject + object + verb + complement, idiomatic in poetry.
segments.s550-g24: خنک is 'cool/refreshing' describing the place/soil just mentioned. آنک is a relative particle 'that which / for whom' linking to the relative clause following. جز is the exclu­sive preposition 'except', تخم نیکی 'the seed of goodness' is the object, and نکشت is the verbal element meaning 'sowed/plant(ed)' in archaic poetry (here forming the clause 'that which sowed nothing but the seed of goodness').
segments.s550-g1: الا‌ای is an archaic vocative interjection ('O, behold') + ای addressing the addressee. خریدار مغز سخن is a nominal phrase in apposition: خریدار (buyer/taker) governs the genitive phrase مغزِ سخن ('the pith/essence of speech'). The whole segment is a direct address to the listener.
segments.s550-g2: دلت = دل + enclitic -ت 'your heart'. برگسل here is an archaic jussive/imperative verb meaning 'detach, unfasten, let be separated' (verb form is lexicalized in poetry). زین is the archaic prepositional compound 'ز + این' = 'from this', and سرای کهن = 'ancient dwelling/house'. The clause therefore commands: 'let your heart be detached from this old abode.'
segments.s550-g3: کجا at the start is a rhetorical/interrogative 'where'. چون من و چون تو = 'like me and like you' (comparative phrases). بسیار دید uses دید (past 'saw, has seen') with بسیار 'many' as its object; word order is literary but straightforward: 'Where has one seen many like me and like you?'
segments.s550-g4: نخواهد here functions as a negative future/modal auxiliary 'will not' (classical use) and همی is an emphatic/progressive particle. با کسی آرمید = 'rest with anyone' (آرمید is perfect/past form used idiomatically after the auxiliary). The clause means 'he/it will never rest with anyone' (generalized statement; the logical subject is omitted).
segments.s550-g5: اگر ... و‌گر sets up a conditional/correlative 'if / or if' ('whether ... or ...'). شهریاری and پیشکار are nominal roles left elliptically (the verb 'to be' is omitted here), giving 'if (you are) a ruler/sovereign or if (you are) an attendant'.
segments.s550-g6: تو ناپایداری is a verbless predicate: 'you [are] unstable/transient' (ناپایداری used as predicate noun). و او پایدار = 'and he [is] lasting/enduring' (copula omitted as is normal in Persian poetry). The contrast (تو ... او ...) is explicit: you are impermanent, he is permanent.
segments.s550-g7: چه ... چه ... here is a correlative pair meaning 'whether ... or ...'. با رنج باشی = 'whether you are with hardship' and چه با تاج و تخت = 'or whether with crown and throne'. باشی is the present subjunctive (literary) 'you are / you may be' used in such correlative clauses.
segments.s550-g8: ببایدت is an archaic fused form meaning 'you must' (ب + باید + ت), with the second-person enclitic -ت. بستن is the infinitive 'to bind/fasten/pack up' and به فرجام رخت is a poetic idiom 'for the end of your garments' where رخت (literally 'garment') with فرجام yields the idiom for final departure; the whole means 'you must prepare/pack/tie up for your final departure.'

sentences.s550-s13: One sign is enough, King Ardashir; when you hear my words, learn (take them to heart).
sentences.s550-s5: If the iron wheel were to melt/consume you, when you grow old it will not spare or caress you either.
sentences.s550-s6: When the beloved cypress is bent into a curve, the narcissus-like (beauties') eyes become tumultuous and sullen.
sentences.s550-s7: That very face, crimson and saffron-colored, when the people's mood becomes light and joyful, becomes esteemed/precious.
sentences.s550-s8: Whether you are a sovereign or a subject, you will find no resting-place except dark earth.
sentences.s550-s9: Where are those great ones with crown and throne? Where are those fortunate horsemen?
sentences.s550-s10: Where are those wise makers/bring-ers? Where are those lofty, warlike chiefs?
sentences.s550-s11: Where are those chosen ancestors of ours? Where are those brave and pure ones of ours?
sentences.s550-s12: All have earth for pillow and a brick; cool is that which sowed nothing but the seed of goodness.
sentences.s550-s1: O buyer of the pith of speech, detach your heart from this ancient dwelling.
sentences.s550-s2: Where has one seen many like me and like you? He will never find rest with anyone.
sentences.s550-s3: Whether (you are) a sovereign or an attendant, you are unstable and he is enduring.
sentences.s550-s4: Whether you live in hardship or with crown and throne, you must bind yourself for your final departure.
